finding_results.notes: Rewrote Viktor's confession to route his guilt through institutional process language rather than delivering it as a clean moral diagnosis. The admission now arrives through committee framing ('I chaired the safety review,' 'documented concerns,' 'deployment parameters fell within acceptable bounds,' 'That was the finding') with a mid-sentence self-catch where he prevents himself from distributing blame to the committee ('which I chaired, so let's not pretend I can distribute that'). The aphoristic closer 'I owe them a floor grate. At minimum' was replaced with a practical acceptance that uses his characteristic vocabulary ('relitigate the framing'). The embezzlement is dispatched quickly and casually ('yes. Let that go out') while the version-three admission gets the convoluted, qualification-heavy treatment his profile prescribes under pressure. No line reads as a standalone aphorism. The calibration-line echo ('I signed off on version three / I reviewed the safety case') was replaced with committee-chair framing that carries the same factual weight without mirroring the example material. Noor's follow-up thought about guilt and safety certification at line 67 remains compatible.

summary: Single finding addressed. Viktor's composed confessional mini-speech was rewritten into his actual institutional register: hedged, committee-framed, and self-aware about its own process language without arriving at a quotable moral conclusion. The factual admissions (embezzlement, version-three approval) and his decision to join the breach are preserved. No other dialogue in the chapter was altered; the remaining speech throughout the chapter already carries appropriate spoken texture and voice separation for this pass.
